LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN MATTHESEN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOHN MATTHESEN'S DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER. CCP 430.41(A) EXPRESSLY REQUIRES A SECOND SUCH MEET AND CONFER AFTER AN AMENDED PLEADING IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER AND DEFENDANT MATTHESEN'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JUNE 2, 2021. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

The only arguable prejudice to defendants if this motion were granted is insufficient time before the trial date to obtain discovery on the new claims and to file summary motions as to the new claims. That prejudice is eliminated by the conditions on the granting of this motion. The strong policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings supports the granting of this motion on the conditions designed to alleviate any prejudice to the defendants.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN MATTHESEN's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT:

The demurrers by defendant John Matthesen (defendant) to the first amended complaint's (FAC) following numbered claims are overruled.
1 (fraudulent transfer): The FAC adequately alleges fraudulent transfer by defendant. (See, e.g., id. at 4:18-23, 6:3-26, 7:12-20.) Factual disputes are raised that are not resolvable by demurrer. (Memo. 4:13-20, 6:3-5.) Defendant cites no authority for his notion that "forms" of entity and receipt need be pled. (Id. at 4:21-24.) Hasso v. Hapke (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 107 does not hold that defendant was free to fraudulently transfer assets because plaintiff had a lien on them; rather, Hasso involved defendants' valid liens.
2 (conspiracy to commit fraudulent transfer): Defendant repeats arguments rejected above and concedes that a fraudulent-transfer defendant may be vicariously liable under a conspiracy theory. The FAC's pleading of conspiracy as a "claim" can be addressed by jury instruction and the verdict form at trial.
3 (intentional interference with contract/prospective economic advantage): Defendant again ignores the FAC's allegations. (See, e.g., id. at 4:18-24, 5:1-5, 6:3-11, 7:3-6, 7:12-16.) The fraudulent transfer was a wrong in addition to the interference. Factual disputes are raised that are not resolvable by demurrer. (Memo. 10:1-10.)
4 (conspiracy to interfere with contract/prospective economic advantage): Defendant repeats arguments rejected above and concedes that a defendant may be vicariously liable under a conspiracy theory. The FAC's pleading of conspiracy as a "claim" can be addressed by jury instruction and the verdict form at trial.
5 (UCL): This case does not involve deceptive conduct in the sale and purchase of securities. (Overstock.com v. Gradient Analytics, Inc. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 688, 715.) Consumer deception is not required of all UCL claims. (Podolsky v. First Healthcare Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 632, 647.)
Defendant's requests for judicial notice are denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
